description_long: This helicopter features a compact, futuristic design with a robust, angular fuselage and a heavily armored appearance. The cockpit is enclosed by a large, tinted, multi-pane window that wraps around the front and sides, providing wide visibility. The wings are short and thick, each housing a circular ducted fan or rotor, suggesting a VTOL (vertical takeoff and landing) capability. The rear of the helicopter is dominated by a large, turbine-like exhaust and reinforced tail section, with no visible tail rotor. The exterior is finished in a dark, metallic color scheme with weathered, industrial accents and yellow highlights. The landing gear consists of two sturdy, downward-extending skids integrated into the lower fuselage.
description: A compact, armored helicopter with a futuristic design, large tinted cockpit windows, and short wings with ducted fans. The exterior is dark metallic with yellow accents and robust landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, tinted cockpit window, angular nose, and both ducted fans embedded in the wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated cockpit, side profile of the ducted fan, and the downward-extending landing skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the large turbine exhaust, reinforced tail, and both wing-mounted ducted fans.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit profile, fan, and landing skid.
attribute_rotor_blades: Main rotor blades are not visible; instead, the helicopter features two large, circular ducted fans embedded in the wings, likely with multiple internal blades made of metal or composite material. The fans are dark with yellow highlights and appear to be about 1 meter in diameter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; the helicopter appears to use a ducted fan or jet propulsion system at the rear instead of a traditional tail rotor.
attribute_landing_skids: Landing skids are robust, angular, and extend downward from the lower fuselage. They are metallic, dark in color, and appear reinforced for heavy landings, with no visible crossbars or footpads.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has a large, multi-pane window wrapping around the front and sides, with a blue-green tint and dark framing. The windows are angular and provide wide visibility.
